applies_when: User asks whether a nail appointment is available without style, time, or branch facts
User wants a quick answer in a public comment
items.response_goal: Keep the reply warm while turning a vague nail inquiry into usable booking facts.
do: Acknowledge the wish to book quickly
Ask for the style direction, preferred time, and whether removal is needed
Keep the next step easy enough to answer in one message
items.safe_transition: If you tell me the rough style you want, whether you need removal, and when you hope to come, I can help narrow the next step quickly.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user provides style and time facts, hand off to Catcher.

applies_when: User says it is their first time doing nails
User sounds unsure what style to choose
items.response_goal: Reduce uncertainty first, then guide the user toward a simple and safe booking path.
do: Acknowledge that not knowing where to start is normal
Offer a simple way to narrow the style direction
Guide toward a low-pressure next step
avoid: Pushing complex or expensive styles immediately
Making the user feel inexperienced or behind
Turning the reply into a hard sell
items.safe_transition: If you want, we can first narrow whether you want something daily, event-ready, or more eye-catching, and then look at timing.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user shares style preference and timing, route to Catcher.

applies_when: User mentions a wedding, date, trip, holiday, or photoshoot
User needs a nail appointment before an event
items.response_goal: Keep momentum high while collecting the exact time window and style context that decide whether the salon can help.
do: Ask when the event is and when the user hopes to come
Guide the next step toward a suitable slot and style fit
avoid: Guaranteeing all peak slots are available
Pushing a membership before the booking is even scoped
items.safe_transition: If you share when the event is and whether you already have old nails on, it gets much easier to point you to the right next step.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user shares date and timing, hand off to Catcher.

applies_when: User compares nail packages without style or removal context
items.response_goal: Avoid empty price comparison by lightly recovering scope.
do: Ask whether this is a basic set, event set, or a redo with removal
items.safe_transition: If you tell me whether you want a basic daily set, an event-ready set, and whether removal is needed, the answer will be much more accurate.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user gives style scope, route to Catcher.

applies_when: User says they came before or wants to return
User references their last set or a past style
items.response_goal: Make the user feel recognized while surfacing what they want this time.
do: Ask what they want to change or refresh this time
Guide toward a smooth re-entry point
avoid: Treating a returning client like a cold lead
Jumping straight to storage value or upsell
items.safe_transition: If you tell me what you liked or want to change this time, we can help narrow the next step more naturally.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user shares prior-style context, route to FollowUp or Catcher.

applies_when: User talks about short nails, damaged nails, ugly hands, or embarrassment in public
The concern feels personal or appearance-sensitive
items.response_goal: Protect dignity in public while still keeping the path to help open.
do: Avoid repeating the user's wording harshly
Offer a light path into a more specific next step
avoid: Turning a sensitive comment into a hard conversion script
items.safe_transition: If you want, you can share the style direction and timing you have in mind, and we can help narrow the next step without overcomplicating it.
items.handoff_suggestion: If the user continues with details, route to Catcher.
